## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with a video about recording your sales offer. Obviously, the sales offer portion of your webinar is vital and you want to get it just perfect. Maybe even have a chance to edit it to make sure it's perfect, perfect. So one option is to actually pre-record your sales offer. Then just hit play at that point in your webinar. Now obviously you have to do this totally seamlessly, both at the beginning of that and at the end so your audience doesn't realize what you've just done with them. Now if you're going to do this, you're going to want to test it a bunch of times and make sure that you've got it working the way you want to. And if you're going to do that, there's a logical place where you'll want to end it. So my recommendation is if you choose to do this, start it so do the pivot live. Then hit record so it goes from the product or mission section all the way down to this is right for you. At that point at the end of that is where you come back on live again seamlessly. They shouldn't even hear or notice that something just changed. And so then you do the frequently asked questions, the offer questions, everything from there to the end is done live. So not everyone can get away with this. Not everyone is fluid enough to do this. But I invite you to consider this. And over time, you may want to so let me tell you how I how I learned about this. I was working with the guy to do a webinar to my list. He's done he probably does 200 webinars a year. He probably does $5 million out of those webinars a year. And he was telling me that this is the way he does all of his webinars. His webinar is always live. And then at the end of the pivot, he hits record, he hits play. And it just plays. And then he comes back in for the question and answer time at the end. And I heard that and I said, why would you do that? And he said, because I want it perfect. I want to have no variables. So I've taken the very, very best job I possibly can do at selling and put that into that recording. And so then I'm never at the mercy of losing my train of thought or going too long on one

## [03:04 - 03:41]

section and having to shorten up another one or something like that, because it's always exactly the same through the offer portion. And he said, and you know what? It's made me millions. That's why I do it. Well, you want to do this? Maybe I wouldn't until you've done 20, 30, 50 different sales offers and learned to really nail it. You need to get good at it before you try to record it and make that something that you use over and over again. But it just wanted to throw it out, something to consider, because it could make all the difference in the world.
